methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Mindgrove Technologies is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Shashwath TR and Sharan Srinivas J, having raised ₹85 Cr (~$10M) to date. The valuation assessment draws from a private comp universe of 8 frontier-stage semiconductor firms, with 4 directly applicable peers at the Series A stage: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. The median valuation across these 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables is $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a 25.3x median price-to-sales multiple, but cannot be applied to Mindgrove without disclosed revenue. The peer-median approach yields $1.5B as the central implied value.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.3B to $1.7B, centered at $1.5B. This reflects the median positioning within a heterogeneous peer set—notably wide given the $4.5B spread between Etched and Rain AI. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the cohort, capital deployment trajectory post-Series A, and whether Mindgrove's product focus (not detailed in available data) aligns with higher-valuation peers like Etched or lower-quartile comparables.
